observation: Task 832 ran a chore to verify the existing drizzle-orm SQL injection vulnerability already tracked in issue #832. All 7 pipeline stages completed with 0 retries, and PR #833 was successfully shipped. However, the verify stage reported FAIL with TypeScript typecheck errors in .next/dev/types/validator.ts (PagesPageConfig constraint violations), yet the pipeline proceeded to ship anyway. This suggests the verify stage failures are informational rather than blocking.
patternMatch: This matches a known pattern from previous runs where pipeline verification failures do not block deployment. The system shipped PR #833 despite verify-stage typecheck failures, consistent with observation #4224 documenting that pipeline verification stage does not block deployment despite failures.
suggestion: Configure the pipeline to block shipping when verify stage fails with typecheck errors, or add a conditional check that prevents autofix/ship stages from running when verify reports FAIL status.

Add a check that reads the verify stage exit status and halts with an error when verify reports FAIL, preventing automatic shipment of code that failed validation. The verify stage should only report FAIL for files within the task scope, and ship should not accept FAIL status regardless of mode.

pipelineFlaw.issue: Verify stage FAIL status does not prevent pipeline progression to ship stage. Verify reported FAIL (date formatting test failures, lint errors in register.tsx) yet ship stage completed with 0 retries and shipped code.
pipelineFlaw.evidence: All 7 stages completed with 0 retries. verify.md shows test failures (expected '1/15/2024, 12:30 PM' to contain '10') and lint errors (setState in effects, module variable assignment) in register.tsx — outside task scope. ship.md shows completed, not skipped. This is the fifteenth consecutive run where verify FAIL does not block ship.

observation: Task 260409-221218 (Add dark mode toggle to header component) completed all 7 pipeline stages with 0 retries each (~394s duration, full mode). Verify stage reported FAIL with date formatting test failures ('expected 1/15/2024, 12:30 PM to contain 10', 'expected 1/1/2100 to contain 2099') and lint errors (setState synchronously within effect, module variable assignment). Ship stage correctly reported 'skipped — local mode, no issue number', avoiding accidental shipment. However, this skip is coincidental and does not fix the structural defect — verify FAIL continues to not block progression to ship in non-local-mode runs.
patternMatch: This is the sixteenth consecutive run (832, 841, 260409-111824, 840, 842, 844, two 419-reruns, 847, 846, 858, 863, 874, 260409-215133, 260409-220738, and now 260409-221218) exhibiting the identical verify-to-ship gate flaw: verify stage reports FAIL due to test failures or lint errors, yet the pipeline proceeds through review-fix to ship. Fifteen prior retrospectives recommended implementing a verifyExitCode conditional gate in the ship stage template. The ship stage skip due to local mode is correct behavior but is coincidental — it does not resolve the structural lack of a blocking mechanism at the verify-to-ship boundary.
suggestion: This pattern has now been documented 16 consecutive times across two days without implementation of the recommended fix. Escalate beyond a recommendation: create a P0 bug ticket tagged 'pipeline-gate-flaw' with label 'urgent' to add a verifyExitCode check in the ship stage template that halts pipeline execution when verify reports FAIL. The ship stage template configuration change is a one-line addition that prevents this entire class of silent quality failures from reaching shipped code.

observation: Task 260409-222402 completed all 7 pipeline stages in ~433s with minimal retries (only review required 1 retry). The task correctly identified that JWT validation already exists in auth middleware and the plan found existing implementations. However, verify stage reported FAIL with date formatting test failures and lint errors in register.tsx (setState synchronous calls, module variable assignment), yet the pipeline proceeded through review-fix and ship without blocking. This is the seventeenth consecutive run exhibiting the identical verify-to-ship gate flaw — verify FAIL status does not halt pipeline progression. Review passed after 1 retry with corrected RGB values, and ship correctly skipped due to local mode, but the structural defect remains: code that fails verification should not reach ship at all.

build.error:
Exit code 1
warn: CPU lacks AVX support, strange crashes may occur. Reinstall Bun or use *-baseline build:
  https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/releases/download/bun-v1.3.11/bun-darwin-x64-baseline.zip
Error: Invalid MCP configuration:
mcpServers.github: Does not adhere to MCP server configuration schema
mcpServers.filesystem: Does not adhere to MCP server configuration schema

observation: Task 2223-260413-211041 failed at the build stage with exit code 1 after 42s in full mode. The build stage failed with an Invalid MCP configuration error stating that both mcpServers.github and mcpServers.filesystem do not adhere to the MCP server configuration schema. This is a new failure mode distinct from the 19 consecutive verify-to-ship gate flaw runs — the pipeline did not reach verify stage, review, or ship at all. CPU AVX warning also appeared, indicating possible environment issues. The retry logic worked (build retried once), but the retry did not resolve the MCP configuration error. The task scope was adding retryWithBackoff utility to utils/retry.ts.
patternMatch: This is a new failure pattern not seen in the 19 prior consecutive verify-to-ship gate flaw runs. The previous 19 runs all exhibited verify reporting FAIL followed by successful progression to ship. This run failed at build due to MCP configuration, never reaching the verify stage. The build stage is treating MCP configuration errors as non-retryable structural failures rather than recoverable setup issues.
suggestion: Fix the MCP server configuration schema for mcpServers.github and mcpServers.filesystem — the configuration format does not match what the MCP server runtime expects. Validate the MCP server configuration format in the pipeline environment before the build stage executes, and add retry logic or a more informative error message when MCP configuration is invalid rather than allowing a silent exit code 1 with no diagnostic output.

pipelineFlaw.issue: MCP server configuration validation failure causes build to exit with code 1, but no diagnostic output explains what schema the configuration must follow
pipelineFlaw.evidence: build stage output: 'Error: Invalid MCP configuration: mcpServers.github: Does not adhere to MCP server configuration schema mcpServers.filesystem: Does not adhere to MCP server configuration schema' — the error provides no details about the expected schema format or which specific fields are malformed. The build stage retries once (1 retry noted) but the retry also fails, indicating the configuration issue is not transient.
